events.label: reasoning
events.text:
>
{"decision": "fail", "risk": "high", "work_stage": "in-progress", "component_conditions": ["installed-defective", "non-compliant"], "percent_complete": 50, "value": 0, "sound_source": "", "confidence": 0.95, "event_time": 0, "rate": 0, "order": [], "workable": [], "findings": ["Click-type torque wrench used upside down, invalidating torque application angle and reading accuracy", "Torque wrench calibration status unknown/uncalibrated, so applied torque cannot be verified against OEM spec", "Fastenings performed with uncalibrated or misused wrench are non-compliant and may be under- or over-torqued", "No documented evidence that torque values meet engineering specification"], "actions": ["Stop work on affected fasteners immediately", "Quarantine or mark all fasteners torqued with the suspect wrench since last known-good calibration", "Remove and re-torque affected fasteners with a calibrated torque wrench used correctly", "Verify current calibration sticker/certificate for the torque wrench before reuse", "Train operator on proper torque wrench orientation and pre-use checks", "Record torque values per standard work and quality gate requirements"], "rationale": "Using a click wrench upside down changes the lever arm and loading geometry, producing inaccurate torque. An uncalibrated wrench cannot provide a traceable, spec-compliant torque value. In a quality-gated production environment, this creates a high risk of joint failure, warranty issues, or safety-critical loosening. The only compliant path is to fail the condition, halt affected work, and re-torque with a calibrated tool used correctly.", "references": ["OEM/engineering specs", "ISO 6789 (torque tool calibration)", "ANSI/ASME B18.2.6 / applicable fastener standards", "Company standard work / quality gate procedures"]}
